questions.question: What is the Ribbon in Microsoft Word?

options.option: The toolbar at the top with tabs containing all formatting and editing commands
options.comment: The Ribbon replaced menus in Office 2007. Tabs (Home, Insert, Layout) group related commands.

options.option: The scrollbar on the right side of the document
The page margin visible around the document text area
The bottom status bar showing page count and word count

questions.question: What is the keyboard shortcut to create a new document in Word?

options.option: Ctrl + N
options.comment: Ctrl+N opens a new blank document. Ctrl+O opens an existing file. Ctrl+S saves the current file.

options.option: Ctrl + D
Ctrl + B
Ctrl + W

questions.question: What is the default file format when saving a Word document?

options.option: .docx
options.comment: .docx is the modern Word format. .doc is the legacy format. .pdf is a non-editable export format.

options.option: .doc
.pdf
.txt

questions.question: What does the 'Quick Access Toolbar' in Word allow you to do?

options.option: Access frequently used commands (Save, Undo, Redo) with one click
options.comment: The QAT sits above the Ribbon and can be customised to show your most-used commands.

options.option: Change the document theme and font instantly
Open a mini web browser within Word
Run a grammar check on the entire document

questions.question: The 'Word Count' feature in Word is found under which Ribbon tab?

options.option: Review tab
options.comment: Review > Word Count shows word, character, paragraph, and line statistics.

options.option: Home tab
Insert tab
View tab

questions.question: What does 'Print Layout' view in Word show you?

options.option: How the document will look when printed, including margins and page breaks
options.comment: Print Layout is the default view. 'Read Mode' removes editing; 'Draft' hides headers/footers.

options.option: Only the text without any formatting or images
A zoomed-in view of selected text
The document as it would appear in a web browser

questions.question: What does the Bold formatting option do to text?

options.option: Makes the text heavier/darker for emphasis (shortcut: Ctrl+B)
options.comment: Bold = Ctrl+B. Italic = Ctrl+I. Underline = Ctrl+U.

options.option: Makes the text larger by one font size
Changes the text colour to black
Adds a border around the selected text

questions.question: What is 'justified' text alignment?

options.option: Text that stretches to align flush with both the left and right margins
options.comment: Justified alignment creates clean edges on both sides, common in newspapers and formal documents.

options.option: Text aligned to the left margin only
Text centred between the left and right margins
Text aligned to the right margin only

questions.question: What is the benefit of using 'Heading' styles (Heading 1, Heading 2) in Word?

options.option: It creates a document structure used by the Navigation Pane and Table of Contents
options.comment: Styles apply consistent formatting AND enable navigation, TOC generation, and accessibility.

options.option: It increases the heading text size by exactly 10 points
It locks heading text so it cannot be accidentally edited
It automatically numbers all paragraphs in the document

questions.question: To change the font size of selected text, you should:

options.option: Select the text, then type the size in the Font Size box on the Home tab
options.comment: You can also use the up/down arrows beside the Font Size box, or Ctrl+Shift+> / Ctrl+Shift+<.

options.option: Right-click and choose 'Resize Text'
Go to Page Layout and change the text scale
Press Ctrl+F and type the new font size

questions.question: What does 'line spacing' control in a Word document?

options.option: The vertical space between lines of text in a paragraph
options.comment: Common line spacings: 1.0 (single), 1.5, 2.0 (double). Set via Home > Line and Paragraph Spacing.

options.option: The horizontal gap between individual characters
The margin size around the entire page
The number of characters that fit on one line

questions.question: To remove all manual formatting from selected text and revert to the default style:

options.option: Press Ctrl + Space (or click 'Clear All Formatting' on the Home tab)
options.comment: Clear All Formatting removes bold, colour, font changes — anything applied on top of the base style.

options.option: Press Ctrl + Z until all formatting is undone
Select the text and press Delete, then retype it
Go to File > Reset Document

questions.question: What is the keyboard shortcut to create a bulleted list in Word?

options.option: There is no shortcut — use the Bullets button on the Home tab
options.comment: The Bullets button is on the Home tab, Paragraph group. You can also type a hyphen then space for a quick list.

options.option: Ctrl + L
Ctrl + Shift + B
Alt + B

questions.question: To add a new row at the bottom of a Word table quickly, you should:

options.option: Press the Tab key when the cursor is in the last cell of the table
options.comment: Tab in the last cell adds a new row automatically — the fastest way to extend a table.

options.option: Right-click the table and select 'Add Row Above'
Press Enter in the last cell
Go to Insert > Table and draw a new row

questions.question: What does 'merging cells' in a Word table do?

options.option: Combines two or more cells into a single larger cell
options.comment: Merge cells are used for headers that span multiple columns. Right-click > Merge Cells.

options.option: Copies the content of one cell into all adjacent cells
Converts the table into a plain bulleted list
Locks the selected cells so they cannot be edited

questions.question: How do you increase the indent level of a bulleted list item?

options.option: Press the Tab key while the cursor is in the list item
options.comment: Tab increases indent (creates sub-bullet). Shift+Tab decreases indent to go back up a level.

options.option: Press Ctrl + Right Arrow
Press the Spacebar five times before the bullet
Click the Increase Font Size button on the Home tab

questions.question: Which type of list is best for instructions that must be followed in a specific order?

options.option: Numbered list
options.comment: Numbered lists show sequence. Bulleted lists are for unordered items where order doesn't matter.

options.option: Bulleted list
Table with two columns
Indented paragraph without numbers or bullets

questions.question: What does 'Paragraph spacing' (Before/After) control in Word?

options.option: Extra space added above or below a paragraph, separate from line spacing
options.comment: Paragraph spacing is the gap between paragraphs. Line spacing is within a paragraph. Both are in the Paragraph settings.

options.option: The left and right indentation of the entire paragraph
The minimum number of lines a paragraph must have before a page break
Whether paragraph text is single-spaced or double-spaced

questions.question: What is the default page size in Microsoft Word for most regions?

options.comment: A4 is the standard in most of the world. US Letter (8.5 x 11 inches) is the US default.

options.option: Letter (8.5 x 11 inches)
Legal (8.5 x 14 inches)

questions.question: What is a page break in Word used for?

options.option: To force content to start on a new page regardless of how much text is above
options.comment: Insert > Page Break (Ctrl+Enter) is cleaner than pressing Enter repeatedly.

options.option: To add a horizontal line across the middle of the current page
To split the document into two separate files
To change the page orientation for that single page only

questions.question: Where does a 'Header' appear in a Word document?

options.option: At the top of every page, above the main body text
options.comment: Headers repeat on every page. They typically contain document title, date, or company name.

options.option: On the first page only, above the title
In the left margin alongside the document text
Below the last paragraph, before the footer

options.comment: Insert > Page Number inserts a field that updates automatically as the document grows.

options.option: Type the page number manually in each footer
Use Find & Replace to add numbers throughout the document
Click the page counter in the status bar and drag it to the footer

questions.question: What does 'Landscape' orientation mean for a page?

options.option: The page is wider than it is tall (horizontal layout)
options.comment: Landscape = wide. Portrait = tall. Useful for tables, charts, or wide spreadsheets in Word.

options.option: The page is taller than it is wide (vertical layout)
The page has no margins — content fills the entire area
The page has a background image or gradient

questions.question: Where are Margin settings found in Microsoft Word?

options.option: Layout tab > Margins (or Page Setup dialog)
options.comment: Layout (or Page Layout) tab contains margins, orientation, size, and columns.

View tab > Page Setup > Borders
Review tab > Restrict Editing > Margins

questions.question: What is the difference between 'Save' and 'Save As' in Word?

options.option: Save updates the current file; Save As creates a new copy with a different name or format
options.comment: Use Save As to rename a file, change its format (e.g. to PDF), or save to a new location.

options.option: Save sends the file by email; Save As saves it to OneDrive
They are identical — both overwrite the current file
Save As is only available in the paid version of Office

questions.question: To export a Word document as a PDF file, you should:

options.option: File > Save As > change file type to PDF
options.comment: Word can export directly to PDF. You can also use File > Export > Create PDF/XPS.

options.option: Copy all text and paste it into Adobe Acrobat
Print the document and scan it back as a PDF
Rename the file from .docx to .pdf in File Explorer

questions.question: What does Print Preview show you?

options.option: How the document will look on paper before you print it
options.comment: Print Preview (included in the Print dialog under File > Print) lets you check layout before wasting paper.

options.option: The print queue showing documents waiting to print
Printer settings such as ink level and paper size
A preview of how the document would look as a PDF

questions.question: To send a Word document to someone so they can edit it, the best method is:

options.option: Share via OneDrive and grant Editor permission
options.comment: OneDrive sharing allows real-time collaboration. Email attachments create separate copies that diverge.

options.option: Print the document and hand it to them
Export as PDF and send it by email
Convert to plain text (.txt) and email it

questions.question: What is AutoSave in Microsoft Word?

options.option: A feature that automatically saves your document to OneDrive as you type
options.comment: AutoSave is enabled for files stored on OneDrive/SharePoint and saves every few seconds.

options.option: A feature that saves the document to a USB drive automatically
A scheduled backup that runs once per hour
A Word setting that creates a copy every time you open the file

questions.question: To print only pages 3 and 5 from a 10-page Word document, you enter in the Pages field:

options.comment: Comma separates individual pages. Use a hyphen for a range: 3-5 prints pages 3, 4, and 5.

options.option: 3-5 (this prints 3, 4, and 5 only)
3/5 (page fraction notation)

questions.question: What is a 'cell' in Microsoft Excel?

options.option: The individual box at the intersection of a row and a column
options.comment: Each cell has a unique address — e.g. B3 means column B, row 3. Click a cell to select it.

options.option: A group of worksheets within a workbook
The entire row of data in a spreadsheet
The formula bar at the top of the spreadsheet

questions.question: What is the name of a single Excel file?

A Spreadsheet Document
A Cell Matrix

questions.question: What is the cell reference for the cell in column C, row 7?

Row7Col3

questions.question: To quickly navigate to cell A1 in a large Excel sheet, press:

questions.question: What happens when you type a number in a cell formatted as 'Text'?

The number is stored correctly and works in all formulas

Right-click a cell and choose 'Expand Column'

questions.question: What does applying a 'Border' to a cell do in Excel?

options.comment: Cell borders are cosmetic. They are found in the Font group (Home tab) or the Format Cells dialog.

options.option: A fraction (15 divided by 7 divided by 2025)
A text string with no special meaning

Applies a gradient to the entire worksheet background

questions.question: What does the SUM function do in Excel?

Calculates the average of a range
Finds the largest number in the range

questions.question: What does the AVERAGE function calculate?

options.option: The arithmetic mean (sum divided by count) of a range of numbers
options.comment: =AVERAGE(B1:B20) adds all values and divides by the number of cells containing numbers.

options.option: The middle value in a sorted list of numbers
The most frequently occurring value in a range
The difference between the highest and lowest values

questions.question: What is AutoFill in Excel used for?

options.comment: The fill handle is the small square at the bottom-right of a selected cell. Drag it to fill a range.

options.option: Automatically inserting data from another spreadsheet
A tool that completes half-typed text in a cell
Filling all empty cells with a chosen value at once

questions.question: Which formula calculates the total of cells B2 through B10?

options.option: Pie chart
Doughnut chart
Bubble chart

Go to File > New > Chart and select data manually

questions.question: What does the VLOOKUP function do?

options.option: Searches for a value in the first column of a range and returns a value from another column
options.comment: =VLOOKUP(lookup_value, table_range, col_index, [exact_match]). V = Vertical.

options.option: Counts all cells that match a given value
Adds values in a column that meet a certain condition
Validates data entries against a lookup table

questions.question: In =VLOOKUP(A2, B:D, 3, FALSE), what does the 3 refer to?

options.option: Return the value from the 3rd column of the lookup range (column D)
options.comment: The column index (3) tells VLOOKUP which column to pull the result from within the table range.

options.option: Find the 3rd row in the lookup range
Repeat the lookup 3 times to check for errors
Round the result to 3 decimal places

questions.question: What does FALSE as the last argument in VLOOKUP mean?

options.option: An exact match is required (returns #N/A if not found)
options.comment: FALSE = exact match. TRUE = approximate match (requires data sorted ascending). Always use FALSE for IDs or codes.

options.option: The function will hide #N/A errors automatically
The lookup is case-sensitive
The search direction is reversed from bottom to top

questions.question: The IF function =IF(C2>50, "Pass", "Fail") will return:

options.option: "Pass" only if C2 equals exactly 50
"Fail" if C2 is greater than 50
An error because text values cannot follow a number comparison

questions.question: What error does VLOOKUP return when it cannot find the lookup value?

options.option: #N/A

options.option: #VALUE!
#REF!
#DIV/0!

questions.question: How do you handle a VLOOKUP that returns #N/A when a match is not found?

options.option: Wrap it in IFERROR: =IFERROR(VLOOKUP(...), "Not found")
options.comment: IFERROR catches any error and returns your custom fallback value instead of showing the error.

options.option: Add FALSE to the end of the formula to suppress errors
Change the column index to 0 to return a blank
Sort the lookup column in descending order before running VLOOKUP

questions.question: What is the purpose of 'Slide Background' formatting in PowerPoint?

options.option: Sets the colour, gradient, texture, or image behind all slide content
options.comment: Right-click a slide > Format Background to customise it. Changes can apply to one or all slides.

options.option: Sets the border colour around the slide edges when printed
Controls the default font colour for all text boxes
Defines the animation that plays when the slide loads

questions.question: What is a slide 'Transition' in PowerPoint?

options.option: A visual effect that plays when moving from one slide to the next
options.comment: Transitions (Fade, Push, Wipe, etc.) are set per slide in the Transitions tab.

options.option: An animation that makes text fly onto the slide from the side
The time delay before a slideshow starts automatically
A hyperlink that jumps to another slide when clicked

questions.question: What is an 'Entrance' animation in PowerPoint?

options.option: An animation that controls how an object appears on the slide
options.comment: Animations: Entrance (how it appears), Emphasis (while visible), Exit (how it disappears).

options.option: The transition effect when the presentation is first opened
A sound effect that plays when a slide is advanced
The motion path an object follows across the slide

questions.question: Where do you manage the order and timing of multiple animations on a slide?

options.option: The Animation Pane (Animations tab > Animation Pane)
options.comment: The Animation Pane lists all animations in order and lets you set timing, delays, and triggers.

options.option: The Slide Sorter view
The Slide Master
The Transitions dialog box

questions.question: To make an animation start automatically after the previous one ends, set it to:

options.option: After Previous
options.comment: Start options: On Click (manual), With Previous (simultaneous), After Previous (sequential).

options.option: On Click
With Previous
Auto Start

questions.question: When should you use animations in a professional presentation?

options.option: Sparingly — only when they help explain or reveal information progressively
options.comment: Too many animations distract from the content. Simple reveals are professional; flashy effects are not.

options.option: On every single object on every slide to make it dynamic
Only on the title slide as a welcome effect
Never — professional presentations should never use animations

questions.question: How do you apply the same transition to all slides at once?

options.option: Select a transition in the Transitions tab, then click 'Apply to All'
options.comment: 'Apply to All' saves time by setting a consistent transition throughout the entire presentation.

options.option: Select all slides in Slide Sorter, then double-click the transition
Set the transition on the Slide Master
Use Ctrl+A to select all slides, then press the transition key

questions.question: What is 'Presenter View' in PowerPoint?

options.option: Shows your notes and next slide privately while the audience sees only the slideshow
options.comment: Presenter View requires two monitors (or a projector). Audience sees the slideshow; you see your notes.

options.option: A mode that locks the presentation so the audience cannot advance slides
A view that shows the presentation as a web page
A recording mode that captures audio and video of your presentation

questions.question: What keyboard shortcut advances to the next slide during a slideshow?

options.option: Press the Right Arrow key, Space bar, or Enter
options.comment: Multiple keys advance slides. The Left Arrow or Backspace goes back to the previous slide.

options.option: Press Ctrl+N to go to the next slide
Press Tab to cycle through slides
Click anywhere on the screen always advances the slide

questions.question: To export a PowerPoint presentation as a PDF, go to:

options.option: File > Export > Create PDF/XPS (or File > Save As > PDF)
options.comment: Exporting as PDF preserves fonts and layout and is ideal for sharing a non-editable version.

options.option: Print the slides and scan them back as a PDF
Copy all slides and paste them into Adobe Acrobat
Rename the .pptx file to .pdf in File Explorer

questions.question: What is the purpose of 'Speaker Notes' in PowerPoint?

options.option: To write reminders and talking points visible only to the presenter during the show
options.comment: Speaker Notes appear in Presenter View and in the notes panel below the slide in Normal view.

options.option: To add captions that the audience sees at the bottom of each slide
To store the presentation script for automatic text-to-speech reading
To record audio commentary that plays during the slideshow

questions.question: What does pressing the 'B' key during a slideshow do?

options.option: Displays a black screen (blanks the screen) to pause attention on you
options.comment: B = black screen. W = white screen. Useful when you need the audience to focus on you, not the slides.

options.option: Goes back to the previous slide
Opens the annotation toolbar for drawing on the slide
Starts broadcasting the presentation over the internet

questions.question: To share a PowerPoint presentation so colleagues can view but not edit it:

options.option: Export as a PDF or share via OneDrive with View Only permission
options.comment: PDF removes editability completely. OneDrive View permission prevents edits while enabling online viewing.

options.option: Convert the file to a .docx format before sharing
Rename the file to .ppsx so it opens directly in slideshow mode
Password-protect the file with a 4-digit PIN
